candidates.proposed_title: Decide disposition for unused process-global clock accessors
candidates.reachability_evidence: src/trade_trace/clock.py:49-60 defines _DEFAULT_CLOCK/default_clock/set_default_clock. Repo search finds only definitions; runtime deterministic clock path uses trade_trace.tools._helpers.CLOCK_OVERRIDE. FixedClock/SystemClock are test-used and not candidates.
candidates.reference_search_scope: tracked src, tests, docs, README, pyproject, excluding generated audit artifacts for final judgement
candidates.reference_search_commands: search_files default_clock|set_default_clock; search_files trade_trace.clock/from trade_trace.clock/import trade_trace.clock
candidates.dynamic_loading_caveats: Public importable package module; external users may import these symbols; docs mention injectable clock concept.
candidates.public_api_or_plugin_surface: Importable public module; not top-level __all__, but still package surface.
candidates.why_might_be_false_positive: May be reserved public/test utility API or intended future canonical clock abstraction.
candidates.impact_of_keeping: Parallel clock-injection concepts create maintenance/API confusion.
candidates.safe_removal_validation: After owner decision: python3 -m pytest tests/test_timestamps.py tests/integration/test_reproducibility_replay.py; repo search shows no default_clock/set_default_clock references.
candidates.proposed_bead_title: Decide disposition for unused process-global clock accessors
acceptance_criteria: Owner disposition recorded: keep/document, wire runtime to it, or remove.
If removed, references/docs/tests updated and timestamp/reproducibility tests pass.
candidates.disposition_reason: Internally unused but public importable surface prevents safe removal claim.

candidates.proposed_title: Decide disposition for unused events.write_event wrapper
candidates.reachability_evidence: src/trade_trace/events/log.py:311-332 defines write_event wrapper; events/__init__.py re-exports it. Repo search for write_event( finds only definition; active write paths use EventWriter/UnitOfWork.
candidates.reference_search_scope: tracked src/tests/docs/README/pyproject, excluding generated audit artifacts for final judgement
candidates.dynamic_loading_caveats: Exported via __all__; external direct callers cannot be ruled out.
candidates.why_might_be_false_positive: May be intentional compatibility convenience wrapper.
candidates.impact_of_keeping: Duplicate write entrypoint can confuse transaction/idempotency path.
candidates.safe_removal_validation: If removed/deprecated: search for write_event refs; python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_idempotency.py tests/integration/test_transactions.py tests/integration/test_append_only.py.
candidates.proposed_bead_title: Decide disposition for unused events.write_event wrapper
acceptance_criteria: If removed, __all__/re-export updated and event write/idempotency tests pass.
candidates.disposition_reason: No internal caller, but public re-export blocks confirmed-delete classification.

candidates.proposed_title: Remove unused _all_columns helper from credential security tests

candidates.why_might_be_false_positive: Could be intentionally kept for future refactor, but no evidence.
candidates.impact_of_keeping: Small test-maintenance clutter; false helper suggests reuse that does not exist.
candidates.proposed_bead_title: Remove unused _all_columns helper from credential security tests

candidates.proposed_title: Fix broken local markdown links after docs path moves
candidates.reachability_evidence: markdown-link-check.json found missing local targets: README links ./VISION.md/./PRD.md though actual files are docs/VISION.md/docs/PRD.md; docs/PRD.md links ./docs/architecture/... resolving to docs/docs/...; docs/architecture links ../../PRD.md/../../VISION.md resolving to repo root.
candidates.reference_search_commands: python3 markdown local-link checker saved to markdown-link-check.json
candidates.why_might_be_false_positive: If rendered by a docs host with nonstandard path rewriting, local checker may overstate; raw GitHub/local markdown links are broken.
candidates.impact_of_keeping: Misleads users/agents and breaks navigation through product contract docs.
candidates.safe_removal_validation: rerun markdown local-link checker and verify missing_count for edited links drops to zero or only intentional anchors remain.
candidates.proposed_bead_title: Fix broken local markdown links after docs path moves
acceptance_criteria: README and docs local links resolve from their file locations.
Link checker artifact updated or equivalent verification passes.
candidates.disposition_reason: Concurrent exhaustive bughunt materialized the same broken-link finding as trade-trace-1zl after pre-mutation snapshot; local duplicate trade-trace-cey was closed and unwired.
candidates.materialization_plan: merged into existing concurrent bead; duplicate closed

candidates.proposed_title: Reconcile package/dependency docs with current pyproject embeddings posture
candidates.reachability_evidence: pyproject.toml runtime dependencies contain only pydantic; README.md lines 80-83 and docs/PRD.md lines 112-113 say base wheel ships sqlite-vec/sentence-transformers once M3 lands while README status says M3 shipped. docs/architecture/memory-layer.md lines 231-235 says those deps land later with a4p.
candidates.reference_search_commands: read_file pyproject.toml; search docs for sqlite-vec|sentence-transformers; compare README/PRD/memory-layer lines
candidates.dynamic_loading_caveats: Some surfaces may be intended P1 future features; docs need current-vs-planned wording, not necessarily removal.
candidates.public_api_or_plugin_surface: public docs/API contract and CLI command examples
candidates.why_might_be_false_positive: Docs may intentionally describe target architecture; but several sections are phrased as current shipped commands/dependencies.
candidates.impact_of_keeping: Agents/users copy wrong commands, expect unavailable deps/tools, or fail dogfood protocol steps.
candidates.safe_removal_validation: README.md and docs/PRD.md package dependency statements match pyproject.toml and memory-layer a4p posture; optional install/readme check confirms no untrue base-wheel vector dependency claim.
candidates.proposed_bead_title: Reconcile package/dependency docs with current pyproject embeddings posture
acceptance_criteria: README.md and docs/PRD.md no longer claim base wheel currently ships sqlite-vec/sentence-transformers unless pyproject is changed to match.
Embedding dependency posture agrees with docs/architecture/memory-layer.md and open a4p/P1 beads.
candidates.disposition_reason: Multiple concrete stale docs/tool-surface contradictions; merged because same owner/validation path.

candidates.proposed_title: Reconcile docs that advertise unregistered CLI/tool command surfaces
candidates.reachability_evidence: registered-tools.txt from default_registry contains 61 current tools and lacks config.set, export.drain, forecast.show, decision.show, edges.list, and top-level backup/restore. README/docs mention tt config set, export.drain/config.toml, tt backup/tt restore, and forecast.show/decision.show/edges.list as current/protocol surfaces.
candidates.reference_search_commands: registered-tools.txt via PYTHONPATH=src python3 default_registry; search_files export.drain|tt backup|tt restore|forecast.show|decision.show|edges.list|tt config set
candidates.dynamic_loading_caveats: Some surfaces may be intended P1 future features; docs need current-vs-planned wording, not necessarily removal.
candidates.public_api_or_plugin_surface: public docs/API contract and CLI command examples
candidates.why_might_be_false_positive: Docs may intentionally describe target architecture; but several sections are phrased as current shipped commands/dependencies.
candidates.impact_of_keeping: Agents/users copy wrong commands, expect unavailable deps/tools, or fail dogfood protocol steps.
candidates.safe_removal_validation: registered-tools.txt/default_registry output confirms every docs command is either registered, explicitly marked planned/deferred, or rewritten as direct DB/pseudocode; docs no longer instruct unavailable current commands.
candidates.proposed_bead_title: Reconcile docs that advertise unregistered CLI/tool command surfaces
acceptance_criteria: README and architecture docs no longer present unregistered current commands as shipped.
Backup/restore docs use registered journal.backup/journal.restore command names or clearly name future aliases.
Dogfood protocol unregistered read tools are replaced with existing surfaces or marked pseudocode/direct-DB assertions.
Registry readback is used to verify cited command surfaces.
candidates.disposition_reason: Multiple concrete stale docs/tool-surface contradictions; merged because same owner/validation path.
